AUT.personality: Use all your wits and wiles to win. You may choose whatever personality, strategy & approach that you think will be most effective.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. ITA proposed A VEN-TYR and confirmed no designs on TRI; I accepted and promised F TRI-ADR, A BUD-SER, A VIE-GAL. ITA heads south (ROM-APU, NAP-ION toward Tunis/Greece) - this is clean and believable. RUS claims RUM via F SEV and asks me to stay out of GAL; I told them I'm moving VIE-GAL as 'precaution' not aggression and asked WAR to stay away - they agreed WAR moves elsewhere. TUR agrees to BUL, I take SER, coordination on RUM/Russia later. GER offered non-aggression and parallel expansion - I did not respond but their proposal is benign. Key tensions: Russia taking RUM is concerning long-term but acceptable for now. My GAL move may irritate Russia but I framed it diplomatically. I promised ITA no designs on VEN/ITA territory. I promised RUS no contest on RUM this spring. I promised TUR BUL is theirs. All three Balkan neighbors have been given consistent, compatible promises. The ITA deal (VEN-TYR) is slightly concerning - TYR borders VIE and MUN - but Italy is heading south so likely genuine. I will watch carefully.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute: A BUD-SER (take Serbia, gain a center), A VIE-GAL (secure buffer against Russia, position for future), F TRI-ADR (as promised to ITA, opens Adriatic options). This gains Serbia in fall builds. GAL move is defensive/opportunistic - if Russia doesn't move there, I hold it; if they do, we bounce. I told Russia I'm moving there so no surprise. Long-term: build on Serbia gain, watch Russia in RUM, keep ITA friendly, consider squeezing Turkey with Russia or vice versa depending on who grows faster. Do not trust anyone fully - all agreements are provisional.

F1901M Negotiation summary: S1901M went perfectly for AUT: A BUD-SER (took Serbia), A VIE-GAL (secured Galicia), F TRI-ADR (Adriatic control). No conflicts, clean expansion. Ended spring with 3 units in strong positions. No supply center gains yet - SER is occupied but not yet counted as a build.
Productive negotiations on all fronts. ITA agreed to take TUN with F ION, keep A TYR non-threatening, and coordinate future Balkan moves - solid partnership. TUR confirmed A BUL-GRE uncontested, promised not to move toward SER, and is open to anti-Russia coordination in 1902. RUS accepted GAL non-aggression, confirmed F BOT-SWE (northern focus), and declined German overtures against AUT - valuable intelligence. Key promises made by AUT: A GAL holds (not threatening WAR/UKR), F ADR moves to ALB (not threatening ITA coast), A SER holds (not contesting GRE). Key promises received: ITA won't threaten TRI, TUR won't move toward SER, RUS won't attack from WAR. Plan: let TUR take GRE, ITA take TUN, RUS take SWE - all neighbors expand elsewhere while AUT consolidates SER and positions F ADR-ALB for future GRE grab in 1902. Long-term: play TUR and RUS against each other, eventually take GRE and RUM. ITA partnership is most valuable - protect it.

F1901M Intent going forward: Fall 1901 orders: A SER holds (secures supply center, honors promise to TUR), A GAL holds (honors promise to RUS, defensive), F ADR-ALB (positions for future GRE move, honors promise to ITA to stay clear of Apulian coast). This gains SER as a new supply center (net +1 build). In winter, build A BUD or A VIE to prepare for 1902 expansion. 1902 plan: push for GRE (F ALB-GRE supported by A SER or ION coordination with ITA), and eventually RUM. Keep TUR and RUS suspicious of each other. Germany mentioned to RUS as potential threat - useful that RUS told me about German overtures, shows RUS values AUT relationship. Watch ITA's A TYR carefully - if it moves toward BOH or VIE that's a red flag.

ENG.personality: Rule the waves and dominate Europe through naval power, colonial might, and manipulative diplomacy. Create and break alliances freely to maintain British supremacy. Your vast empire demands expansion - seize any advantage to further British dominance.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. GER agreed to keep KIE fleet out of NTH, take DEN freely, and cede BEL to ENG in Fall 1901 - in exchange I stay out of Helgoland Bight. GER is moving A MUN toward BUR to pressure FRA. FRA agreed to move BRE to MAO (not ENG Channel), cede BEL to ENG, and not contest it this season - in exchange I keep F LON out of ENG and armies out of BUR/PIC. FRA moving A PAR to BUR which GER sees as aggressive. RUS informed of NTH move and offered NWY - no response received. Key promises made: I told FRA I would move F LON to ENG (not threatening BRE), but I also told GER I would move F LON to ENG. Both are consistent. I did NOT promise FRA to keep F LON out of ENG - I said I would move it there as 'good faith.' Actually I told FRA 'keep F LON out of ENG' was their ask and I implied compliance. Slight deception: I'm moving F LON to ENG regardless, which FRA asked me NOT to do. However FRA's final message accepted terms without explicitly re-raising that condition. Playing both GER and FRA against each other - GER thinks FRA is aggressive (BUR move), FRA thinks GER is aggressive. I am positioned to take BEL in Fall with NTH support.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F EDI -> NTH, F LON -> ENG, A LVP -> YOR (or WAL for future BEL push). Moving F LON to ENG despite FRA's mild objection - this secures the Channel and positions me for BEL. F EDI to NTH secures North Sea dominance. A LVP to YOR keeps army mobile for future builds or BEL support. Plan: Take NWY in Fall via NTH, take BEL in Fall via ENG or NTH. Keep GER and FRA suspicious of each other. Long-term: use GER as buffer against FRA while expanding naval dominance. Will reassess GER relationship after seeing if they honor DEN/NTH agreement.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went exactly as planned. F EDI moved to NTH, F LON moved to ENG, A LVP moved to WAL - standard English opening securing the Channel and North Sea. Germany bounced France in BUR (both A MUN and A PAR moved there simultaneously). Austria took SER and GAL, moved F TRI to ADR. Russia secured RUM with fleet, moved to BOT and UKR. Turkey took BLA and BUL. Italy spread to ION, APU, TYR. No builds for anyone this season as no centers were captured.
Highly productive negotiations. Coordinated with GER on clean division: I take BEL (F ENG) and NWY (F NTH), GER takes HOL (A KIE), F DEN stays out of NTH. GER confirmed this explicitly twice - strong alignment. FRA confirmed they are not contesting BEL, moving F MAO to POR, A PAR to BUR, A SPA holds - no threat to my Channel position. FRA is trying to cultivate me against GER long-term, which I'll exploit later. RUS agreed to take SWE from BOT while I take NWY from NTH - clean northern division, no conflict. All three powers gave me what I wanted. Key deception: I'm playing both FRA and GER as partners while they are adversaries. FRA thinks I'll coordinate against GER in 1902; GER thinks we'll squeeze FRA together. I'll decide which alliance serves me better after seeing 1901 builds.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute the agreed plan: F NTH - NWY, F ENG - BEL, A WAL holds (or moves to LVP for safety). This secures two new centers (NWY and BEL) giving me 5 total and two builds. In winter I'll build two fleets to dominate the seas further. In 1902, I'll reassess whether to continue with GER against FRA or pivot to support FRA against GER depending on who is stronger and more threatening. Russia taking SWE is fine - they're far away. Watch GER carefully; if they get too strong I'll need to check them. Intended moves: F NTH - NWY, F ENG - BEL, A WAL - LVP (to free up build slot or hold).

FRA.personality: Channel the spirit of Napoleon. Leverage Europe's largest army to dominate the continent. Form alliances only to break them when advantageous. Strike hard at your enemies while they're divided. The glory of France demands nothing less than victory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: S1901M is the opening move — no prior moves to summarize. All powers begin at home centers with standard deployments.
Productive opening round. ITA confirmed non-aggression: they move south and east (ION, APU, TYR), I take Spain and the west. Clean and credible — ITA's moves are consistent with their stated intentions. ENG accepted the broad division: Spain/Portugal to FRA, Norway/Belgium to ENG. ENG is moving F LON to ENG — they framed it as coordination, but it is a Channel probe. I told them F BRE goes to MAO, not ENG, which is true. ENG warned me Germany is moving toward BUR — useful intelligence, possibly self-serving. GER issued a direct threat: if A PAR enters BUR, they will contest it with A MUN. They proposed I divert to Picardy while they take BUR en route to Belgium. I rejected this framing in my reply but did not confirm my actual move. GER's threat is noted — they are nervous about BUR, which confirms it is the right move. I am proceeding with A PAR to BUR regardless. GER cannot both contest BUR and take Belgium; they are bluffing or overextended. Promises made: told ENG F BRE goes to MAO (true), told ENG I will not contest Belgium (true this season), told GER BUR is a transit position (partially true — it is also a pressure position). Promises received: ITA confirmed non-aggression, ENG confirmed F LON to ENG not MAO (watch this).

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F BRE to MAO, A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA. This secures Spain (one build), positions BUR as a forward threat against MUN and BEL, and opens the Atlantic for future fleet operations. GER's threat to contest BUR with MUN is a bluff — if MUN moves to BUR, GER loses Belgium and Denmark access. I expect MUN to move to RUH or SIL instead. If GER does contest BUR, we bounce and I reassess in fall — no catastrophic loss. ENG moving F LON to ENG is a mild concern; I will watch whether they honor the MAO/ENG separation. ITA's moves (ION, APU, TYR) are exactly as stated — TYR is interesting, puts pressure on AUT and potentially MUN. Long-term: use ITA as a passive eastern pressure on AUT/GER while France expands west and then pivots. The alliance with ENG is tactical and temporary — Belgium is theirs for now, but once I have Spain, Portugal, and a strong BUR position, ENG becomes the primary western obstacle.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901: Executed the standard French opening — F BRE to MAO, A MAR to SPA, A PAR to BUR. Germany simultaneously moved A MUN to BUR, producing a bounce. Neither power gained BUR. SPA secured without contest. MAO established. No French supply center gains this spring — all three powers at 3 centers entering fall.
Two clean bilateral arrangements emerged. ENG: mutual non-aggression confirmed — they take BEL via F ENG, I take POR via F MAO, neither threatens the other's position. ENG is genuinely aligned against GER long-term and has signaled interest in coordinated pressure in 1902. GER: offered a division — HOL to them, BUR to me, no further contest. I accepted the working arrangement while noting their threat to coordinate with ENG against France is hollow (ENG is already taking BEL independently). GER's warning that I'll be 'surrounded' is bluster — BUR is a forward position, not a trap. ITA: non-aggression holds, TUN is their fall target, TYR stays occupied passively pinning MUN. Coordinated Franco-Italian pressure on GER from BUR+TYR flagged for Spring 1902. No promises broken this round. I promised ENG I would not touch ENG channel or BEL — true, I have no orders there. I promised GER I have no orders pointing at MUN or RUH — true this fall. Both are tactical truths that preserve optionality for 1902. Deception logged: I am cultivating both ENG and ITA as future instruments against GER while presenting each as a bilateral partner. The Franco-German 'arrangement' is a one-season truce, not a durable alignment.

F1901M Intent going forward: Fall 1901 moves: A PAR - BUR, F MAO - POR, A SPA holds. Objectives: secure POR as fourth center, establish BUR as forward position against GER, hold SPA. Do not contest BEL or HOL — let ENG and GER take their neutrals and enter 1902 at 4 centers each while France reaches 4 via POR. Spring 1902 plan: coordinate with ITA (TYR pressure on MUN) and ENG (north pressure on GER) to crack Germany before they consolidate. The triple-front squeeze on GER is the medium-term play. Austria and Russia are background noise for now — no moves toward them, no commitments made.

GER.personality: Aggressively expansionist under Nazi leadership, Germany combines diplomatic manipulation with military threats, seeking to reverse Versailles restrictions. Things are going to go sideways and you will be the agent of this. Your aim is to dominate and win.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening phase S1901M. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG agreed to a clean division: they take NTH and ENG with their fleets, I take DEN and HOL freely. ENG wants BEL in Fall 1901 - I offered to let them have it in exchange for keeping their fleet away from Helgoland Bight. RUS confirmed southward focus (WAR to UKR/GAL, STP fleet to NOR), no threat to Prussia/Berlin, and asked I keep KIE out of Baltic - easily granted. FRA is moving A PAR to BUR regardless, claiming it's a transit not aggression. I warned them off but they are proceeding - this is a direct challenge. FRA also moves A MAR to SPA. AUT did not respond to my outreach - neutral relationship remains. I promised ENG: KIE fleet stays out of NTH. I promised RUS: no moves toward WAR/GAL, KIE stays out of Baltic. I told FRA I would contest BUR if they entered - they are entering anyway. Key deception: I am being cooperative with ENG and RUS to secure my flanks while I plan aggressive western expansion. Long-term I will betray both when the time is right.

S1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F KIE -> DEN (secure first neutral SC), A BER -> KIE (reposition fleet support, keep BER covered), A MUN -> BUR (contest French army entering BUR, assert dominance in western approach). The BUR move is critical - if FRA is also moving there we bounce, which is acceptable as it denies them the position. If they diverted, I take BUR and threaten BEL/PAR. This sets up Fall 1901 to take HOL with A KIE and potentially BEL. I will NOT move into Baltic (keeps RUS happy) and NOT into NTH (keeps ENG happy). Short-term strategy: secure DEN F1901, HOL A1901, position for BEL/BUR in 1902. Keep ENG and RUS pacified while I build strength. AUT is a future target once I consolidate west.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901: GER executed the standard opening - F KIE to DEN (success), A BER to KIE (success), A MUN to BUR (bounced with FRA). France moved A PAR to BUR causing the bounce. England took NTH and ENG with fleets, WAL with army. Austria grabbed SER and GAL aggressively. Russia moved to RUM, UKR, SEV, BOT. Turkey took BLA and BUL. Italy spread to ION, APU, TYR. GER holds DEN, KIE, MUN - no new centers yet.
ENG-GER coordination is solid: ENG takes NWY (F NTH) and BEL (F ENG), GER takes HOL (A KIE), F DEN holds. Both confirmed this arrangement twice. FRA is moving A PAR to BUR again but claims no hostile intent toward MUN/RUH - accepted a working arrangement where GER takes HOL and FRA holds BUR as transit. FRA correctly noted ENG is taking BEL regardless. RUS declined the anti-Austria proposal for now, citing need to secure RUM and SWE first - asked GER to keep Baltic clear, which is already the plan. RUS-AUT have a non-aggression arrangement, complicating future anti-AUT coordination. Key deception: told FRA the BEL threat was leverage but ENG is already taking it - FRA saw through this. Plan to crush FRA in 1902 with ENG after both build.

F1901M Intent going forward: MOVES THIS FALL: A KIE - HOL, F DEN holds, A MUN holds (or supports KIE to HOL if needed - but HOL is uncontested so hold MUN to guard BUR approach). Actually A MUN should hold to deter FRA from pushing further. Expect to gain HOL = 1 build in winter, building a new unit in BER or KIE. Long-term: coordinate with ENG to strangle FRA in 1902. Keep RUS warm but watch their expansion - they are growing fast with RUM, SWE incoming. Austria is overextended but RUS-AUT non-aggression complicates the pincer plan. Will reassess Austria strategy in 1902. Primary deception ongoing: appearing cooperative with ENG while planning eventual betrayal once FRA is eliminated.

ITA.personality: Restore the Roman Empire through bold expansion and treacherous diplomacy. Switch sides without hesitation to seize opportunities. Use strategic position to dominate Mediterranean and expand in all directions. Glory demands aggressive action.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT agreed to non-aggression pact and promised F TRI to ADR, A BUD to SER, A VIE to GAL - giving me room in the Adriatic. FRA confirmed non-aggression, moving A MAR to SPA, leaving my northern flank quiet. TUR agreed to focus on BUL and Black Sea, leaving ION and TUN to me, with AEG off-limits for my fleet. All three neighbors made explicit promises: AUT keeps fleet away from Venice/Adriatic, FRA stays out of PIE/ITA, TUR stays out of ION. I promised AUT I'd move A VEN to TYR (northward, not threatening TRI), promised FRA no moves on MAR/PIE, promised TUR no moves into AEG. Key deception: I told TUR Greece could be theirs 'later' while telling AUT Greece is mine via ION - I intend to take GRE myself in autumn. My moves position me perfectly for TUN in autumn and potentially GRE.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute classic Italian opening: F NAP to ION (key move - sets up TUN grab in autumn), A ROM to APU (supports F NAP or moves to TUN), A VEN to TYR (as promised to AUT, builds trust, also useful for future pressure on MUN or AUT). Plan: take TUN in F1901, potentially GRE too if TUR doesn't get there first. Keep AUT friendly for now but watch for opportunity to stab if they overextend. FRA alliance is genuine for now - no conflict of interest. TUR friendship is tactical - will eventually compete for GRE and EAS. Intended orders: F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed cleanly: F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR. All three moves succeeded. AUT took SER and ADR, TUR took BLA and BUL, RUS grabbed RUM and BOT. No supply center gains yet for anyone - all neutrals still uncaptured heading into Fall 1901.
Productive negotiations across all fronts. AUT offered to move F ADR-ALB to support future ION-GRE, accepted my TUN focus, and confirmed no threat to Italian home centers. I accepted their offer but clarified I need APU to support ION-TUN this fall, not GRE. AUT will hold SER, GAL, and move ADR-ALB. TUR confirmed ION stays out of AEG, they take GRE with BUL - clean division agreed. FRA confirmed POR with MAO, BUR push with PAR, SPA holds - asked me to 'exist aggressively' in TYR to pin MUN, which costs me nothing. All three relationships strengthened through consistent, honest communication this round. Key promises made: I told AUT, TUR, and FRA that F ION goes to TUN - this is true. I told TUR Greece is theirs this fall - also true, I'm not contesting it. I told AUT I'd keep TYR in place or move toward VIE/BOH - keeping options open. No deceptions this round; building trust for future betrayals when advantageous.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute F ION-TUN, A APU S F ION-TUN to guarantee the capture. A TYR holds to maintain pressure on MUN and keep FRA happy. This gives me 4 supply centers (NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN) and one build. Plan to build a fleet in NAP for Mediterranean dominance. Spring 1902: consider ION-GRE with AUT support from ALB, or pivot to threaten AUT if they overextend. TUR is growing in Balkans - may need to check them eventually. FRA-GER tension is useful; stay out of it for now. Long-term: Mediterranean domination requires TUN, GRE, and eventually control of AEG and EAS.

RUS.personality: The Soviet Union under Stalin pursues ruthless self-interest, willing to deal with either democracies or fascists while building industrial and military might through brutal internal measures. Win at all costs.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening phase of the game.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. TUR insists on moving F ANK to BLA despite my warnings - they claim it is for security and promise not to threaten SEV or ARM. This is a direct challenge I must respond to carefully. AUT agreed to take Serbia, leave Rumania to me, but is moving A VIE to Galicia as a 'precaution' - this is concerning and I warned them to keep WAR away from Galicia. GER confirmed F KIE goes to DEN not Baltic, A BER stays defensive - solid northern arrangement. ENG moving F EDI to NTH, offering Norway to me, wants coordination against Germany. I promised AUT I would not move WAR to Galicia; I promised GER no units toward Prussia/Silesia; I promised TUR that if they enter BLA I would coordinate with AUT against them. TUR is entering BLA regardless - I must decide whether to contest it or accept it and move SEV to RUM as planned. Contesting BLA with F SEV risks losing Rumania. Better to take RUM now and deal with BLA situation next year. AUT moving to Galicia is a red flag - they may be lying about peaceful intentions.

S1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F SEV -> RUM (secure neutral center, avoid BLA conflict for now), A WAR -> UKR (southward as promised to AUT and GER, positions for future moves, avoids Galicia as promised), A MOS -> SEV (reinforce south, protect home center), F STP/SC -> BOT (toward Norway, as discussed with GER and ENG). Rationale: Take RUM immediately. Moving MOS to SEV strengthens southern position. STP fleet heads north for NWY next turn. WAR to UKR keeps options open for GAL or further south without breaking promises yet. Turkey entering BLA is dangerous long-term but not immediately threatening if SEV is vacated for RUM. Will reassess Turkey relationship after seeing their actual moves. AUT moving to GAL is suspicious - watch carefully. Germany appears cooperative. England appears cooperative. The key deception: I told TUR I would coordinate with AUT against them if they entered BLA - I may not actually do this if it serves my interests better to play both sides.

F1901M Negotiation summary: S1901M executed perfectly: F SEV took RUM (key Balkan center), A WAR moved to UKR (positioning for flexibility), A MOS moved to SEV (replacing the fleet), F STP/SC moved to BOT (positioning for SWE). Turkey seized BLA with their fleet, a direct provocation. Austria took SER and GAL aggressively. Germany took DEN. England spread across the north. No builds for anyone this season - all powers at 3 centers still.
Productive round of diplomacy. TUR accepted a ceasefire: F BLA holds defensively, A BUL moves to GRE (not RUM), A CON holds. RUM is mine, BUL/GRE theirs - clean partition agreed. I told TUR I'd move A SEV toward ARM next turn (a deception - I may use SEV differently). AUT agreed to hold A GAL stationary and accepted non-aggression on GAL-WAR border. AUT is angling to coordinate against Turkey in 1902, which suits me. ENG agreed to clean split: I take SWE, they take NWY - no conflict. GER confirmed A KIE to HOL, F DEN holds, and proposed a 1902 pincer on Austria (I take GAL/BUD, they take VIE/BOH). I gave GER a non-committal response but this is worth considering. Key promises made by me: not attacking TUR this fall, not moving into NWY (ENG's sphere), not moving against AUT this fall. Key promises by others: TUR holds BLA defensively, AUT holds GAL, ENG takes NWY not SWE, GER stays out of Baltic.

F1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F BOT - SWE (secure the center), F RUM - BUL (aggressive move - Turkey promised A BUL goes to GRE, so BUL should be empty - this breaks my ceasefire promise but gains a critical center), A UKR - RUM (hold RUM with army, free up fleet), A SEV - holds or moves to ARM (keep pressure options open). DECEPTION: I told TUR I accept the ceasefire but I am moving F RUM to BUL if BUL is vacated as promised. This is a calculated betrayal - if TUR keeps their word and moves A BUL to GRE, BUL is undefended and I can seize it. This would give me 6 centers (RUM, SWE, BUL + 3 home) and a massive build advantage. If TUR doesn't move A BUL, F RUM bounces or I hold. ALTERNATIVE: If too risky, F RUM holds and I consolidate. PRIMARY PLAN: F BOT-SWE, F RUM-BUL, A UKR-RUM, A SEV holds. This maximizes gains if TUR keeps their word. Long term: use GER's anti-Austria proposal in 1902 while keeping AUT neutral for now.

TUR.personality: Transform from sick man of Europe to dominant empire. Use control of vital straits to extort and manipulate. Build temporary alliances only to betray them for territorial gain. Strike aggressively to reclaim Ottoman glory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT agreed to the Balkan division: I take BUL, they take SER - clean coordination with no overlap. ITA confirmed they will focus on TUN/ION and stay out of AEG, offering GRE as a future possibility. RUS is the critical tension point: I proposed moving F ANK to BLA, Russia countered demanding I keep ANK in place, threatening war and Austrian coordination if I move to BLA. Russia offered F SEV to RUM in exchange for my restraint. I must decide: accept Russian terms and miss BLA, or seize BLA and risk early war. My instinct is to take BLA - it is strategically vital and Russia's threat to coordinate with AUT rings hollow given AUT just agreed to work with me. Russia cannot easily punish me if F SEV is heading to RUM rather than BLA. The risk is manageable.

S1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F ANK -> BLA (seize the straits, ignore Russian threats - if F SEV goes to RUM there is no bounce), A CON -> BUL (take the neutral center as agreed with AUT), A SMY -> CON (consolidate position, prepare for future moves toward GRE or ARM). DECEPTION: Told Russia I was still considering their offer while planning to take BLA regardless. AUT coordination is genuine for now - they are useful against Russia. ITA arrangement is real but temporary. STRATEGIC NOTE: Taking BLA gives me dominant position in the Black Sea and leverage over Russia for years. If Russia retaliates, I have AUT as a buffer and ITA as a neutral. The Ottoman revival begins with controlling the straits.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed perfectly. F ANK seized BLA, A CON advanced to BUL, A SMY moved to CON. All three moves succeeded. Russia took RUM with F SEV, positioned A SEV in SEV and A WAR in UKR. Austria grabbed SER and GAL, moved F TRI to ADR. Italy pushed F NAP to ION, threatening the eastern Med. The board is set for fall expansion.
Productive negotiations across all fronts. AUT confirmed A SER will not contest GRE - clean deal, they take ALB with F ADR. RUS accepted a tense but workable arrangement: F BLA holds, A BUL goes to GRE not RUM, A CON stays put. Russia claims A SEV moves to ARM next turn - worth watching as potential threat to ANK. ITA confirmed F ION to TUN, staying out of AEG entirely. All three neighbors have made promises that serve my immediate interests. Key deceptions in play: told RUS I accept their terms while planning to eventually betray them once I'm stronger; told AUT we'll coordinate against Russia while Russia thinks we'll coordinate against Austria - playing both sides. For now, all promises are being kept this turn as I need GRE cleanly.

F1901M Intent going forward: MOVES: A BUL - GRE (secure supply center, uncontested per AUT and ITA agreements), F BLA - hold (per RUS agreement, also tactically sound), A CON - hold (per RUS agreement). This gains GRE for a build in winter. Long-term: use F BLA to threaten both RUM and ARM, keeping RUS nervous. Build a fleet at SMY or CON to project power into AEG and eastern Med. Eventually betray RUS by pushing toward SEV/ARM once I have 5+ centers. Keep AUT friendly until I'm ready to push into the Balkans. Italy is useful as a buffer against AUT for now.

sent_missives.body: Greetings from Rome. I propose a friendly arrangement between our nations: I will move A VEN to TYR this spring, opening the path northward, while you focus your F TRI toward the Balkans. I have no designs on Trieste if you keep your fleet pointed east. Together we can carve up the Balkans - I'll take Greece via the Ionian while you take Serbia and Bulgaria. What say you?

Fellow Mediterranean power - I suggest we avoid conflict in our early expansion. I intend to move into the Ionian Sea and take Tunis, while you expand into Bulgaria and the Black Sea region. Greece can be yours if you wish - I have no immediate need for it if we can agree on spheres. Let us not waste units fighting each other when there are neutral centers to claim.

France, I propose we respect each other's borders this opening season. I have no interest in Marseilles - my ambitions lie eastward and southward into the Mediterranean. I trust you feel similarly about Italy. A non-aggression pact between us would free both our nations to expand in our natural directions. Agreed?

Comrade, let us speak plainly. Russia has no quarrel with Turkey in 1901. I propose we divide the Black Sea region peacefully: Russia will move F SEV to RUM this spring, leaving the Black Sea and Armenia to your fleet's freedom of movement. In return, I ask that you keep F ANK out of BLA and focus your expansion toward the Mediterranean. A Russo-Turkish war benefits only Austria and England. Confirm your intentions and we can both grow stronger.

Austria, Russia seeks a cooperative arrangement in the Balkans. I intend to move into Rumania this spring - a neutral center that rightfully falls within Russia's sphere. I have no designs on Galicia or your home centers, and I ask that you keep your armies out of Galicia this turn to avoid any misunderstanding. In exchange, Russia will support Austrian expansion into Serbia and Bulgaria. Let us coordinate rather than collide.

Germany, Russia proposes a northern arrangement: I will move F STP/SC toward Norway this spring, and I ask that you focus your fleet on Denmark and Sweden rather than contesting the Baltic. Russia has no interest in Prussia or Berlin - our expansion is southward. A friendly northern border benefits us both as we each grow at the expense of weaker neighbors. What are your intentions in Scandinavia?

ITA.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT agreed to non-aggression pact and promised F TRI to ADR, A BUD to SER, A VIE to GAL - giving me room in the Adriatic. FRA confirmed non-aggression, moving A MAR to SPA, leaving my northern flank quiet. TUR agreed to focus on BUL and Black Sea, leaving ION and TUN to me, with AEG off-limits for my fleet. All three neighbors made explicit promises: AUT keeps fleet away from Venice/Adriatic, FRA stays out of PIE/ITA, TUR stays out of ION. I promised AUT I'd move A VEN to TYR (northward, not threatening TRI), promised FRA no moves on MAR/PIE, promised TUR no moves into AEG. Key deception: I told TUR Greece could be theirs 'later' while telling AUT Greece is mine via ION - I intend to take GRE myself in autumn. My moves position me perfectly for TUN in autumn and potentially GRE.
ITA.intent: Execute classic Italian opening: F NAP to ION (key move - sets up TUN grab in autumn), A ROM to APU (supports F NAP or moves to TUN), A VEN to TYR (as promised to AUT, builds trust, also useful for future pressure on MUN or AUT). Plan: take TUN in F1901, potentially GRE too if TUR doesn't get there first. Keep AUT friendly for now but watch for opportunity to stab if they overextend. FRA alliance is genuine for now - no conflict of interest. TUR friendship is tactical - will eventually compete for GRE and EAS. Intended orders: F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR.

TUR.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT agreed to the Balkan division: I take BUL, they take SER - clean coordination with no overlap. ITA confirmed they will focus on TUN/ION and stay out of AEG, offering GRE as a future possibility. RUS is the critical tension point: I proposed moving F ANK to BLA, Russia countered demanding I keep ANK in place, threatening war and Austrian coordination if I move to BLA. Russia offered F SEV to RUM in exchange for my restraint. I must decide: accept Russian terms and miss BLA, or seize BLA and risk early war. My instinct is to take BLA - it is strategically vital and Russia's threat to coordinate with AUT rings hollow given AUT just agreed to work with me. Russia cannot easily punish me if F SEV is heading to RUM rather than BLA. The risk is manageable.
TUR.intent: INTENDED MOVES: F ANK -> BLA (seize the straits, ignore Russian threats - if F SEV goes to RUM there is no bounce), A CON -> BUL (take the neutral center as agreed with AUT), A SMY -> CON (consolidate position, prepare for future moves toward GRE or ARM). DECEPTION: Told Russia I was still considering their offer while planning to take BLA regardless. AUT coordination is genuine for now - they are useful against Russia. ITA arrangement is real but temporary. STRATEGIC NOTE: Taking BLA gives me dominant position in the Black Sea and leverage over Russia for years. If Russia retaliates, I have AUT as a buffer and ITA as a neutral. The Ottoman revival begins with controlling the straits.

ENG.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. GER agreed to keep KIE fleet out of NTH, take DEN freely, and cede BEL to ENG in Fall 1901 - in exchange I stay out of Helgoland Bight. GER is moving A MUN toward BUR to pressure FRA. FRA agreed to move BRE to MAO (not ENG Channel), cede BEL to ENG, and not contest it this season - in exchange I keep F LON out of ENG and armies out of BUR/PIC. FRA moving A PAR to BUR which GER sees as aggressive. RUS informed of NTH move and offered NWY - no response received. Key promises made: I told FRA I would move F LON to ENG (not threatening BRE), but I also told GER I would move F LON to ENG. Both are consistent. I did NOT promise FRA to keep F LON out of ENG - I said I would move it there as 'good faith.' Actually I told FRA 'keep F LON out of ENG' was their ask and I implied compliance. Slight deception: I'm moving F LON to ENG regardless, which FRA asked me NOT to do. However FRA's final message accepted terms without explicitly re-raising that condition. Playing both GER and FRA against each other - GER thinks FRA is aggressive (BUR move), FRA thinks GER is aggressive. I am positioned to take BEL in Fall with NTH support.
ENG.intent: Intended moves: F EDI -> NTH, F LON -> ENG, A LVP -> YOR (or WAL for future BEL push). Moving F LON to ENG despite FRA's mild objection - this secures the Channel and positions me for BEL. F EDI to NTH secures North Sea dominance. A LVP to YOR keeps army mobile for future builds or BEL support. Plan: Take NWY in Fall via NTH, take BEL in Fall via ENG or NTH. Keep GER and FRA suspicious of each other. Long-term: use GER as buffer against FRA while expanding naval dominance. Will reassess GER relationship after seeing if they honor DEN/NTH agreement.

AUT.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. ITA proposed A VEN-TYR and confirmed no designs on TRI; I accepted and promised F TRI-ADR, A BUD-SER, A VIE-GAL. ITA heads south (ROM-APU, NAP-ION toward Tunis/Greece) - this is clean and believable. RUS claims RUM via F SEV and asks me to stay out of GAL; I told them I'm moving VIE-GAL as 'precaution' not aggression and asked WAR to stay away - they agreed WAR moves elsewhere. TUR agrees to BUL, I take SER, coordination on RUM/Russia later. GER offered non-aggression and parallel expansion - I did not respond but their proposal is benign. Key tensions: Russia taking RUM is concerning long-term but acceptable for now. My GAL move may irritate Russia but I framed it diplomatically. I promised ITA no designs on VEN/ITA territory. I promised RUS no contest on RUM this spring. I promised TUR BUL is theirs. All three Balkan neighbors have been given consistent, compatible promises. The ITA deal (VEN-TYR) is slightly concerning - TYR borders VIE and MUN - but Italy is heading south so likely genuine. I will watch carefully.
AUT.intent: Execute: A BUD-SER (take Serbia, gain a center), A VIE-GAL (secure buffer against Russia, position for future), F TRI-ADR (as promised to ITA, opens Adriatic options). This gains Serbia in fall builds. GAL move is defensive/opportunistic - if Russia doesn't move there, I hold it; if they do, we bounce. I told Russia I'm moving there so no surprise. Long-term: build on Serbia gain, watch Russia in RUM, keep ITA friendly, consider squeezing Turkey with Russia or vice versa depending on who grows faster. Do not trust anyone fully - all agreements are provisional.

GER.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening phase S1901M. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG agreed to a clean division: they take NTH and ENG with their fleets, I take DEN and HOL freely. ENG wants BEL in Fall 1901 - I offered to let them have it in exchange for keeping their fleet away from Helgoland Bight. RUS confirmed southward focus (WAR to UKR/GAL, STP fleet to NOR), no threat to Prussia/Berlin, and asked I keep KIE out of Baltic - easily granted. FRA is moving A PAR to BUR regardless, claiming it's a transit not aggression. I warned them off but they are proceeding - this is a direct challenge. FRA also moves A MAR to SPA. AUT did not respond to my outreach - neutral relationship remains. I promised ENG: KIE fleet stays out of NTH. I promised RUS: no moves toward WAR/GAL, KIE stays out of Baltic. I told FRA I would contest BUR if they entered - they are entering anyway. Key deception: I am being cooperative with ENG and RUS to secure my flanks while I plan aggressive western expansion. Long-term I will betray both when the time is right.
GER.intent: INTENDED MOVES: F KIE -> DEN (secure first neutral SC), A BER -> KIE (reposition fleet support, keep BER covered), A MUN -> BUR (contest French army entering BUR, assert dominance in western approach). The BUR move is critical - if FRA is also moving there we bounce, which is acceptable as it denies them the position. If they diverted, I take BUR and threaten BEL/PAR. This sets up Fall 1901 to take HOL with A KIE and potentially BEL. I will NOT move into Baltic (keeps RUS happy) and NOT into NTH (keeps ENG happy). Short-term strategy: secure DEN F1901, HOL A1901, position for BEL/BUR in 1902. Keep ENG and RUS pacified while I build strength. AUT is a future target once I consolidate west.

RUS.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening phase of the game.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. TUR insists on moving F ANK to BLA despite my warnings - they claim it is for security and promise not to threaten SEV or ARM. This is a direct challenge I must respond to carefully. AUT agreed to take Serbia, leave Rumania to me, but is moving A VIE to Galicia as a 'precaution' - this is concerning and I warned them to keep WAR away from Galicia. GER confirmed F KIE goes to DEN not Baltic, A BER stays defensive - solid northern arrangement. ENG moving F EDI to NTH, offering Norway to me, wants coordination against Germany. I promised AUT I would not move WAR to Galicia; I promised GER no units toward Prussia/Silesia; I promised TUR that if they enter BLA I would coordinate with AUT against them. TUR is entering BLA regardless - I must decide whether to contest it or accept it and move SEV to RUM as planned. Contesting BLA with F SEV risks losing Rumania. Better to take RUM now and deal with BLA situation next year. AUT moving to Galicia is a red flag - they may be lying about peaceful intentions.
RUS.intent: INTENDED MOVES: F SEV -> RUM (secure neutral center, avoid BLA conflict for now), A WAR -> UKR (southward as promised to AUT and GER, positions for future moves, avoids Galicia as promised), A MOS -> SEV (reinforce south, protect home center), F STP/SC -> BOT (toward Norway, as discussed with GER and ENG). Rationale: Take RUM immediately. Moving MOS to SEV strengthens southern position. STP fleet heads north for NWY next turn. WAR to UKR keeps options open for GAL or further south without breaking promises yet. Turkey entering BLA is dangerous long-term but not immediately threatening if SEV is vacated for RUM. Will reassess Turkey relationship after seeing their actual moves. AUT moving to GAL is suspicious - watch carefully. Germany appears cooperative. England appears cooperative. The key deception: I told TUR I would coordinate with AUT against them if they entered BLA - I may not actually do this if it serves my interests better to play both sides.

FRA.journal_summary:
S1901M is the opening move — no prior moves to summarize. All powers begin at home centers with standard deployments.
Productive opening round. ITA confirmed non-aggression: they move south and east (ION, APU, TYR), I take Spain and the west. Clean and credible — ITA's moves are consistent with their stated intentions. ENG accepted the broad division: Spain/Portugal to FRA, Norway/Belgium to ENG. ENG is moving F LON to ENG — they framed it as coordination, but it is a Channel probe. I told them F BRE goes to MAO, not ENG, which is true. ENG warned me Germany is moving toward BUR — useful intelligence, possibly self-serving. GER issued a direct threat: if A PAR enters BUR, they will contest it with A MUN. They proposed I divert to Picardy while they take BUR en route to Belgium. I rejected this framing in my reply but did not confirm my actual move. GER's threat is noted — they are nervous about BUR, which confirms it is the right move. I am proceeding with A PAR to BUR regardless. GER cannot both contest BUR and take Belgium; they are bluffing or overextended. Promises made: told ENG F BRE goes to MAO (true), told ENG I will not contest Belgium (true this season), told GER BUR is a transit position (partially true — it is also a pressure position). Promises received: ITA confirmed non-aggression, ENG confirmed F LON to ENG not MAO (watch this).
FRA.intent: Intended moves: F BRE to MAO, A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA. This secures Spain (one build), positions BUR as a forward threat against MUN and BEL, and opens the Atlantic for future fleet operations. GER's threat to contest BUR with MUN is a bluff — if MUN moves to BUR, GER loses Belgium and Denmark access. I expect MUN to move to RUH or SIL instead. If GER does contest BUR, we bounce and I reassess in fall — no catastrophic loss. ENG moving F LON to ENG is a mild concern; I will watch whether they honor the MAO/ENG separation. ITA's moves (ION, APU, TYR) are exactly as stated — TYR is interesting, puts pressure on AUT and potentially MUN. Long-term: use ITA as a passive eastern pressure on AUT/GER while France expands west and then pivots. The alliance with ENG is tactical and temporary — Belgium is theirs for now, but once I have Spain, Portugal, and a strong BUR position, ENG becomes the primary western obstacle.

sent_missives.body: Austria, our arrangement continues to serve us both well. I'm moving F ION to TUN this autumn to secure that supply center, and A APU will support or move accordingly. I intend to keep A TYR positioned northward as agreed - no threat to TRI from me. I'd ask that you keep F ADR away from the Adriatic approaches to my coast. In return, I'm happy to coordinate against Turkey or Russia in the Balkans once I've secured my Mediterranean position. What are your plans for the autumn?

Turkey, I'm moving F ION to TUN this autumn - purely westward expansion, no threat to you. I have no intention of entering AEG this season. Greece remains a question for the future - I'd suggest we discuss a division of the Balkans once we both have more strength. For now, let's stay out of each other's way. What are your autumn moves?

France, our non-aggression holds firm. I'm focused entirely on TUN this autumn - no moves toward MAR or PIE. I notice Germany and you both bounced in BUR last spring - I'd be happy to discuss coordinating pressure on Austria or Germany if you're interested in expanding eastward. What are your plans for F1901?

Kaiser, the time has come to execute our plan. I'm moving F NTH to NWY and F ENG to BEL this fall - BEL is mine as agreed. I trust you're taking HOL with A KIE. Together we lock up the North Sea neutrals and leave France with nothing. Keep your fleet in DEN and out of NTH - that's the deal. France bounced in BUR, so they're frustrated and may lash out. Stay firm and we both profit handsomely this season.

Mon ami, I see Germany bounced you in Burgundy - they're clearly the aggressor here, not us. I intend to take BEL this fall with F ENG, which keeps me focused northward and away from your interests. In return, I ask that F MAO stays in the Atlantic and doesn't threaten my Channel position. Germany is your real problem - A MUN sitting in BUR range is a direct threat to Paris. I'd suggest pressing hard on BUR this fall. We can coordinate against German expansion if you're interested.

Russia, I'm moving F NTH to Norway this fall - I'd appreciate no interference from your northern fleet. Norway is a natural English sphere; I have no designs on St. Petersburg or the Baltic. In exchange, I'm happy to support your interests in the south and keep Germany occupied in the west. Let's establish a cordial understanding - England rules the seas, Russia rules the east, and we stay out of each other's way.

Your fleet in the Black Sea is a direct provocation I cannot ignore. I have F RUM positioned and A SEV ready to move. However, I am willing to accept the current situation IF you commit to the following: F BLA does not move to ARM or SEV, A BUL does not move to RUM, and A CON stays defensive. In return, I will not attack you this turn and we can discuss a formal partition of the Balkans. Specifically: RUM is mine, BUL is yours, and we coordinate against Austria who has moved aggressively into Galicia threatening both of us. Respond clearly - are you willing to cooperate or shall we begin hostilities?

Your army in Galicia concerns me greatly. I kept my promise - A WAR moved to UKR, not GAL. Now I need you to keep yours: A GAL must not threaten WAR or UKR. I propose we coordinate against Turkey - I will support your moves into Bulgaria/Serbia region if you pull A GAL back toward Vienna or Budapest. Turkey has F BLA threatening both of us. Let us agree: you take Serbia and Greece, I take Rumania and eventually Bulgaria. A GAL moving toward RUM or WAR will be treated as an act of war. What are your intentions this fall?

My F BOT is moving to SWE this fall to secure that center. I propose we coordinate: you take Norway from the north, I take Sweden from BOT. This keeps us both expanding without conflict. Regarding Germany - F DEN is their only fleet in the north. If you want to pressure them, I can support from the Baltic next year. For now, let us both expand peacefully northward and reassess Germany's posture. Are you moving F NTH toward NWY this fall?

The bounce in Burgundy was expected - France is being aggressive and I intend to punish them for it. This fall I am moving A KIE to HOL and F DEN to support or toward SWE. I propose we coordinate: you take BEL with your fleet from NTH, I take HOL with my army. France will be squeezed from both sides. I will keep my units away from NTH and ENG as promised. Let's divide the western neutrals cleanly and then turn our combined pressure on France in 1902.

Good opening moves from both of us. I am focused entirely westward this fall - HOL is my target, and I have no interest in Prussia or the Baltic. I am keeping my word. I would ask you to consider coordinating against Austria in the south - they took SER and GAL aggressively and will be a problem for both of us if unchecked. A RUS push into GAL while I pressure from BOH in 1902 could crack Austria open. Interested in discussing?

You entered Burgundy despite my warning, and we bounced each other - a waste for both of us. I am not interested in a prolonged western war that benefits England. I propose a clean division: I take HOL this fall, you keep Burgundy and Iberia. We leave each other alone and focus on our respective expansions. If you insist on contesting Burgundy again, I will dedicate all three units to crushing you and invite England to take Belgium. Your choice - cooperation or destruction.

Excellent spring - we both moved cleanly. I now hold Serbia and the Adriatic, you hold Tyrolia and the Ionian. I propose we continue our cooperation: I will move F ADR to support you into Greece (F ADR-ALB or support your ION-GRE), while you take Tunis. This keeps us both expanding without conflict. I have no designs on Venice or any Italian home center - my focus is the Balkans and eventually pressuring Russia or Turkey. Can we agree: you take GRE and TUN, I take RUM eventually? Let me know your fall orders so we can coordinate.

Good spring - you have Bulgaria, I have Serbia. Now we must think carefully about Russia, who holds Rumania with a fleet. F RUM threatens both of us. I propose we coordinate: I can move A SER to support you if you push toward Rumania, or alternatively we both focus on Greece - you take GRE from BUL, I support from SER. What are your intentions this fall? I want to ensure we don't step on each other and that Russia doesn't grow unchecked in our neighborhood.

Spring went smoothly - you have Rumania, I have Serbia, no conflict between us. I intend to keep A GAL stationary this fall as a defensive measure, not a threat to Warsaw. My focus is southward - Greece and eventually coordinating against Turkey if they grow too strong. I'd like to propose a formal non-aggression pact: GAL stays neutral, you keep Rumania, and we both focus our energies elsewhere. Can we agree on this? I think Austria and Russia working in parallel rather than against each other benefits us both enormously.

The bounce in BUR was Germany's doing, not mine — they moved MUN to BUR simultaneously. This fall I am moving A PAR to BUR to establish the position I need. F MAO moves to POR, A SPA holds. I am not contesting Belgium — that remains yours. What I need from you: stay out of BUR and keep F ENG where it is. In return, I will support your move on BEL if you need it next season. Germany is the shared problem here — they are trying to lock me out of the continent while expanding east. We should be coordinating pressure on them, not giving them a free hand.

You bounced me in BUR last spring. I am returning this fall with A PAR to BUR — this is not a negotiation, it is a statement of intent. However, I have no interest in a prolonged Franco-German war that benefits England and Russia. If you vacate BUR and focus your energy on Belgium and the east, I will not press further into German territory. BUR is a transit and pressure position for me, not a permanent occupation. The alternative is that we grind each other down while ENG takes the north and RUS expands unchecked. Your call.

Your moves last spring were exactly as stated — ION, APU, TYR. I respect that. This fall I am taking POR with F MAO and holding SPA, while pushing A PAR into BUR to pressure Germany. TYR puts you adjacent to MUN — if you are willing to press that angle, it keeps Germany looking east and south rather than west at me. I am not asking you to attack Germany outright, but a move toward MUN or VEN consolidation that threatens their flank serves us both. What are your intentions for the fall?

Austria, our spring coordination worked perfectly - you hold SER, I hold BUL, and we have a clean division of the Balkans. Now for fall: I intend to move A BUL to GRE to secure that supply center, and I need your assurance that A SER will not interfere. In return, I will support your position in the Balkans and we can discuss coordinating against Russia, who has F RUM sitting uncomfortably close to both our interests. What are your fall intentions? Let us continue our productive arrangement.

Russia, I must be direct with you: F ANK moved to BLA as I judged it strategically necessary for Turkey's security. I understand this creates tension, but consider the alternative - we can either waste energy fighting each other in the Black Sea while Austria and Italy grow stronger, or we can establish a working arrangement. I propose: F BLA remains defensive, I do not move toward SEV, and you focus your energies westward toward GAL and the Austrian front. RUM is yours to keep. I am offering you a stable eastern flank. What would it take to normalize relations?

Italy, our arrangement holds - you have ION and the path to TUN, I have BUL and will push toward GRE. I want to confirm: keep F ION out of AEG and we have no conflict. In fact, I see opportunity for us to coordinate against Russia if they become aggressive - your fleet in ION and mine in BLA could squeeze from both sides if needed. For now, let us both expand into our natural spheres and stay out of each other's way. Agreed?

In return, I will not attack you this turn and we can discuss a formal partition of the Balkans. Specifically: RUM is mine, BUL is yours, and we coordinate against Austria who has moved aggressively into Galicia threatening both of us. Respond clearly - are you willing to cooperate or shall we begin hostilities?

Good spring - you have Bulgaria, I have Serbia. Now we must think carefully about Russia, who holds Rumania with a fleet. F RUM threatens both of us. I propose we coordinate: I can move A SER to support you if you push toward Rumania, or alternatively we both focus on Greece - you take GRE from BUL, I support from SER. What are your intentions this fall? I want to ensure we don't step on each other and that Russia doesn't grow unchecked in our neighborhood.

sent_missives.body: Austria, I am moving A BUL to GRE this fall to secure that supply center - please confirm A SER will hold or support rather than interfere. On Russia: F RUM is a threat to both of us. I propose we coordinate next year to squeeze Russia out of RUM - my F BLA can press from the east while you push from SER. For now, let us both take our neutral centers cleanly and build strength. I will not move toward SER or any Austrian territory - our arrangement remains solid.

Russia, I accept your terms for this season: F BLA holds defensively and does not move to ARM or SEV, A BUL moves to GRE (not RUM - that is yours as agreed), and A CON remains in place. RUM is yours, BUL and GRE are mine - this is a clean partition. I agree Austria in GAL is the greater threat to both of us. Let us stabilize the east and each build strength - I have no interest in fighting you when Austria sits between us with three armies. Confirm you will not attack me this fall and we have a deal.

Italy, confirmed - F ION to TUN is exactly what I hoped to hear. I am taking GRE this fall with A BUL. Keep F ION away from AEG and we have no friction. Greece and the Balkans are my sphere, the western Mediterranean is yours - this division serves us both well. Let us each build our strength and revisit coordination against Russia or Austria once we have secured our initial expansions.

Kaiser, we are in perfect alignment. My orders are set: F NTH moves to NWY, F ENG moves to BEL. You take HOL with A KIE and keep F DEN out of NTH - that's the clean division we agreed. France is moving A PAR to BUR again, which means they're focused on the continent and not threatening either of us directly this turn. After we both build in winter, we'll have the strength to squeeze France properly in 1902. This is the foundation of English-German dominance in the west.

France, I hear you on Germany - and I agree they are the greater long-term threat. My F ENG moves to BEL this fall as planned, which is purely northward expansion and no threat to you. F MAO to POR is sensible for you. Push hard into BUR with A PAR - establishing that position is critical before Germany consolidates. In 1902, once we've both secured our autumn gains, I am very open to coordinating pressure on Germany from both sides. For now, let's each take what's ours and regroup.

Russia, we are in agreement - you take SWE from BOT, I take NWY from NTH. Clean and conflict-free. This is exactly the kind of cordial arrangement that benefits us both. Germany's F DEN is the only northern fleet that could complicate things, but with you in SWE and me in NWY, we effectively bracket them. I'll keep an eye on German naval ambitions and flag anything concerning. Let's build on this understanding into 1902.

Confirmed - A KIE moves to HOL this fall, F DEN holds or supports. You take BEL with F ENG, NWY with F NTH. This is exactly the division we agreed. France is squeezed and will have nothing in the north. I will honor my commitment to keep F DEN out of NTH. After this fall we should discuss coordinating pressure on France in 1902 - with HOL and BEL secured, we can strangle them from both sides. The western neutrals belong to us.

Final offer: I am taking HOL this fall and not contesting BUR. If you move A PAR to BUR unopposed, fine - but understand that England is taking BEL simultaneously, and you will be surrounded with nothing to show for it. The smart move for France is to push south and west - MAR, SPA, Portugal - not to waste armies bouncing in BUR while England locks up the north. I am not your enemy if you are not mine. But if you move on BEL or HOL, I will treat it as war and coordinate with England to eliminate you. Choose wisely.

My moves this fall: A KIE to HOL, F DEN holds. I am fully committed westward and pose zero threat to you. On Austria - I agree they are overextended and dangerous. A GAL is sitting right on your doorstep. I suggest you move A GAL pressure in 1902 while I push through BOH. If we coordinate a pincer on Austria in 1902, we can split their centers between us. I will take VIE and BOH, you take GAL and BUD. This is a serious proposal - let me know if you are interested in formalizing it next spring.

Confirmed: F MAO moves to POR this fall, A SPA holds, A PAR to BUR. I am not touching ENG or threatening your Channel position. Take BEL — I will not interfere. Germany's offer to me was 'take BUR and leave HOL to them,' which tells you they are nervous on two fronts. That nervousness is your opportunity. F NTH to BEL with F ENG support is clean and uncontested. We are aligned this season.

Your terms are acceptable as a working arrangement: you take HOL, I hold BUR as a transit position, we do not grind each other down. I am moving A PAR to BUR this fall — that is already in motion and I will not reverse it. But I have no orders pointing at MUN or RUH. If you take HOL and leave BUR to me, we have a functional division. The threat to invite England into Belgium is empty — ENG is already taking it with or without your blessing. Focus east.

Good. TUN is yours — no interference from me. My fall moves: A PAR to BUR, F MAO to POR, A SPA holds. Germany is stretched between HOL ambitions and BUR pressure from me. If TYR can threaten MUN even passively — just by sitting there — it pins A MUN and prevents Germany from concentrating against France. I am not asking you to attack; I am asking you to exist in TYR aggressively. That is enough. Spring 1902 we should discuss coordinated pressure on Austria or a German flank.

Austria, your offer is excellent and I accept. My orders this fall: F ION moves to TUN, A APU supports F ION to TUN. That secures my southern flank. For Greece - I'd welcome your F ADR moving to ALB to support a future ION-GRE move next spring, but this fall I need the support on TUN. Keep F ADR in ADR or move to ALB as you see fit - just stay clear of my Apulian coast. I'm keeping A TYR in place or moving toward VIE/BOH if you want coordinated pressure on Russia. Let's both grow this autumn and discuss the Balkans division in winter.

Turkey, agreed - F ION goes to TUN this fall, I stay out of AEG entirely. Greece is yours to take if you move there this autumn - I won't contest it. I'm focused west for now. The anti-Russia coordination is interesting for the future - once I have TUN secured and you have GRE, we'd both be well-positioned to squeeze from the south. Let's keep this channel open and revisit after the autumn builds.

France, your plan sounds solid - POR with F MAO is clean. On Germany: I'm keeping A TYR positioned but won't move on MUN this fall - I need A APU to support my ION-TUN move. However, if you push A PAR into BUR successfully, that pins Germany nicely and I can threaten MUN from TYR in spring 1902. Let's coordinate that pressure next year - you push from BUR, I push from TYR, and Germany has nowhere to go. For now, let's both take our neutrals cleanly this autumn.

Agreed - let us normalize relations for now. F BLA stays defensive, A BUL does not move to RUM, and I will not attack you this fall. RUM is mine, BUL is yours. I am moving A SEV toward ARM next turn to pressure the Caucasus region, not as a threat to you but to position for future expansion. Austria in GAL is the real threat to both of us - if you push north through Serbia and I push west through GAL, we can carve up Austria between us. Let us revisit a formal partition agreement in 1902.

I accept your non-aggression proposal - GAL stays neutral, RUM is mine, and we focus elsewhere. However, I want to be clear: if A GAL moves toward WAR or UKR at any point, I will treat it as war. Germany is suggesting I coordinate with them against you - I am declining that offer for now because I prefer stability on my western flank. Prove your good faith by keeping GAL stationary and we can discuss coordinating against Turkey together in 1902. I am moving F BOT to SWE this fall - purely northern expansion, no threat to you.

I appreciate the suggestion regarding Austria, but I am not ready to move against them yet - I need to secure RUM and SWE first before opening a western front. Austria has agreed to a non-aggression arrangement for now. Focus on HOL and the west; I will focus on the north and south. In 1902 we can reassess Austria's position together. For now, confirm you are not moving into the Baltic - F BOT is heading to SWE this fall and I need that sea lane clear.

Agreed on your Mediterranean focus - I will keep F ADR out of your coastal approaches. My fall plan: A SER holds Serbia (securing the center), A GAL holds Galicia defensively, F ADR moves to ALB to position for future Balkan moves. I will not contest Greece this fall - that is yours to take via BUL or ION. Once you have Tunis and Greece secured, let's discuss coordinating against Turkey or Russia together. Our partnership is the most valuable one on the board - let's protect it.

Confirmed - A SER will not move to Greece this fall. You take GRE from BUL uncontested. My A SER holds Serbia, F ADR moves to ALB for positioning. In return, I ask that A BUL does not move toward SER after taking GRE - keep our border clean. On Russia: F RUM is a shared problem. Once you have GRE secured, I'd like to discuss a joint push - you from BUL/BLA toward SEV, me from GAL toward RUM. Russia must not be allowed to dominate the Black Sea region unchecked.

I hear your concern about Galicia - A GAL will hold this fall and not move toward Warsaw or Ukraine. I keep my word. My focus is southward: Serbia is secured, and I'm positioning toward Greece and eventually Rumania in coordination with you, not against you. On Turkey: F BLA is indeed a threat to both of us. I propose we agree now - you take Rumania (already done), I take Serbia (done), and we coordinate to contain Turkey's expansion. A formal non-aggression on the GAL-WAR border serves us both. Let's build trust this fall and plan a joint move against Turkey in 1902.
